title: NOC 32209 – Other Practitioners of Natural Healing | GTR Immigration
description: Other Practitioners of Natural Healing under NOC 32209 include a diverse group of holistic and alternative health professionals providing complementary therapies across Canada. With increasing public interest in integrative medicine, preventive care, and wellness services, this TEER 2 occupation continues to see stable demand nationwide. For qualified international practitioners, NOC 32209 may support eligibility under Express Entry and selected Provincial Nominee Programs, offering a pathway toward Canada PR.

duties: Assess clients and recommend herbal treatments
Cultivate herbs and prepare herbal compounds
Use holistic methods including herbal remedies, massage, and nutritional counselling
employment_requirements: Completion of specialized training programs related to the specific natural healing practice
Supervised practical training may be required
Completion of a three-year diploma in homeopathy
Registration for other natural healing professions varies by province and is often voluntary or employer-dependent.
International applicants may need credential recognition depending on province and employer standards.
salary_prospects: Annual income typically ranges from $42,400 to $105,900, particularly for experienced practitioners operating successful private clinics.
job_outlook: Growing consumer interest in preventive healthcare
Rising demand for alternative pain management
Self-employed practitioners in metropolitan areas may experience stronger earning potential.
labour_market_demand: Professionals under NOC 32209 commonly work in:
This positive outlook reflects growing demand for preventive and complementary therapies, particularly in urban provinces such as Ontario, British Columbia, Alberta, and Quebec.
Why Immigrate to Canada Under NOC 32209?
Other Practitioners of Natural Healing benefit from:
Growing wellness and alternative therapy market
Increasing public acceptance of holistic health approaches
Canada’s multicultural healthcare landscape supports diverse traditional and complementary health systems.
pathways: If you are qualified under NOC 32209, you may be eligible for immigration pathways to Canada through Express Entry or Provincial Nominee Programs.
